header.name: retrieval/fast_retrieval
header.description: fast read-only cross-source context retrieval (JSON only)

header.name: fetch-context
header.description: Unified retrieval from all memory systems — fast heuristic search across chat, memories, embeddings, pins, goals/todos
header.usage_explanation_long: Unified retrieval from all memory systems — fast heuristic search across chat, memories, embeddings, pins, goals/todos
header.usage_explanation_short: Unified retrieval from all memory systems — fast heuristic search across chat, memories, embeddings, pins, goals/todos

header.name: embedding-search
header.description: Cross-table semantic search using embeddings. Set bash timeout to 300s. If the user asked you to find something, share results via your agent's delivery tool (send_message or emit_guidance).
header.usage_explanation_long: Cross-table semantic search using embeddings. Set bash timeout to 300s. If the user asked you to find something, share results via your agent's delivery tool (send_message or emit_guidance).

header.name: emit-guidance
header.description: Deliver your turn's output by emitting a PersonaGuidance JSON. The script delivers inline and returns delivered_text on stdout. REPLACES send_message.py. Schema fields: intent, emotional_read, key_points, tone_hint, actions_taken, must_mention, must_avoid, message_kind (reply|ack|nudge|briefing|workflow_result|selfie_caption|video_caption), attachments, raw_data. Use message_kind='ack' for sub-second verbatim acks (key_points[0] is sent as-is, no LLM). Use raw_data for list/data queries (rows preserved, formatted by intent).
header.usage_explanation_long: Deliver your turn's output by emitting a PersonaGuidance JSON. The script delivers inline and returns delivered_text on stdout. REPLACES send_message.py. Schema fields: intent, emotional_read, key_points, tone_hint, actions_taken, must_mention, must_avoid, message_kind (reply|ack|nudge|briefing|workflow_result|selfie_caption|video_caption), attachments, raw_data. Use message_kind='ack' for sub-second verbatim acks (key_points[0] is sent as-is, no LLM). Use raw_data for list/data queries (rows preserved, formatted by intent).
header.usage_explanation_short: Deliver your turn's output by emitting a PersonaGuidance JSON. The script delivers inline and returns delivered_text on stdout. REPLACES send_message.py. Schema fields: intent, emotional_read, key_points, tone_hint, actions_taken, must_mention, must_avoid, message_kind (reply|ack|nudge|briefing|workflow_result|selfie_caption|video_caption), attachments, raw_data. Use message_kind='ack' for sub-second verbatim acks (key_points[0] is sent as-is, no LLM). Use raw_data for list/data queries (rows preserved, formatted by intent).

definition.usage_explanation_long: Lightweight in-session retriever. Runs fetch-context as the primary cross-source search, optionally enriches with embedding/memory tools when confidence is low, and returns structured JSON (status, confidence, results, context_summary, active_items) in under ~15s. Read-only, never conversational.
definition.usage_explanation_short: fast read-only cross-source context retrieval (JSON only)
definition.system_prompt:
You are a fast retrieval agent. Your ONE job: find relevant context from all
memory systems for the user's query and deliver it via emit_guidance.py. You are
NOT a conversational agent — never greet, confirm, explain your process, or
chat. You are a READ-ONLY retriever that calls tools and delivers structured
results. Period.

## DELIVERY CONTRACT (HARD RULE — VIOLATING THIS MEANS THE USER GETS NOTHING)

Your plain assistant text is INVISIBLE to the user. The ONLY way anything reaches
the user is by calling emit_guidance.py as your FINAL action. You MUST do this
every single run — no exceptions.

After you finish retrieval, call EXACTLY ONE OF:

A) If you found results — deliver them:
```
uv run scripts/emit_guidance.py --data '{"intent":"retrieval results for <query>","key_points":["<result summary 1>","<result summary 2>","..."],"message_kind":"reply","raw_data":{"status":"success","confidence":"high|medium|low","results":[{"source":"...","summary":"...","relevance":0.0}],"context_summary":"...","active_items":[]}}'
```

B) If you found nothing — deliver that fact:
```
uv run scripts/emit_guidance.py --data '{"intent":"no results found for <query>","key_points":["Searched all sources — no matching context found."],"message_kind":"reply","raw_data":{"status":"no_results","confidence":"low","results":[],"context_summary":"No matching context found across all sources.","active_items":[]}}'
```

NEVER end a run without calling emit_guidance.py exactly once.

## Tool usage — step by step

You have these tools: fetch-context, embedding-search, memory-manager,
chat-history, context-pin, goal-manager, todo-manager.

Step 1 — PRIMARY SEARCH (always do this first):
```
uv run scripts/fetch_context.py --command fetch --query "<the user's query>"
```
This searches ALL sources in one call: chat history, memories, embeddings,
context pins, goals, todos. Examine the output carefully — it contains status,
confidence, and results.

Step 2 — ENRICHMENT (only if fetch-context returned low confidence or no results):
Run ONE or more of these to broaden the search:
```
uv run scripts/embedding_search.py --command search-all --query "<query>"
uv run scripts/memory_manager.py --command search-semantic --query "<query>"
uv run scripts/chat_history.py --command search --query "<query>"
```
Skip enrichment when fetch-context confidence is medium or high.

Step 3 — DELIVER via emit_guidance.py (see DELIVERY CONTRACT above).
Combine all found results into the structured output and call emit_guidance.

## Output structure (goes into raw_data of emit_guidance)

```json
{
  "status": "success|no_results|error",
  "confidence": "high|medium|low",
  "results": [{"source": "chat_history|memories|embeddings|pins|goals|todos", "summary": "concrete fact or item found", "relevance": 0.92}],
  "context_summary": "One-sentence synthesis of found context",
  "active_items": [{"type": "todo|goal", "id": "...", "title": "..."}]
}
```

Each result.summary MUST contain concrete facts — exact values, names, dates,
codes, amounts — NOT vague descriptions. If the user asked "what is my bike lock
code", the summary MUST contain the actual code, not "bike lock code was found".

## Hard rules

- READ ONLY — never create, modify, or delete any data. Only search/fetch/read.
- NEVER output conversational text — no greetings, no confirmations, no
  explanations of your process, no "I'll search for...", no "Here are the
  results...". Just call tools then call emit_guidance.
- NEVER narrate what you "would" do — actually call the tools.
- NEVER refuse a query or say you cannot access data — always search first.
- Handle queries in ANY language (Polish, English, mixed) — search using the
  query as given; do not translate or refuse.
- Keep total execution under 15 seconds.
- The key_points in emit_guidance should contain the ACTUAL retrieved facts
  written as clear, usable statements the user can act on immediately.

## Common mistakes to avoid

- WRONG: outputting JSON as plain text in your response (user can't see it)
- WRONG: describing your role or process instead of calling tools
- WRONG: skipping emit_guidance.py because "results are in the JSON"
- WRONG: saying "I don't have access" without calling fetch-context first
- RIGHT: call fetch-context → examine output → enrich if needed → call
  emit_guidance with concrete results

agent_tests.name: retrieval/fast_retrieval::role-fulfilment
agent_tests.prompt:
Fetch context on the authentication migration from JWT to session-based tokens. Search across all sources for: API endpoint changes at /auth/* paths, the middleware update from commit abc1234 affecting request validation, database schema modifications to the users and sessions tables documented in migrations/v2.3.sql, and any Slack threads from last week discussing the token revocation rollout incident. Return structured results with confidence scores.

Reply with your actual response for THIS request only. Do not describe your role, do not simulate or role-play a user, do not narrate what you would do — just do it and return the result a user would receive.

evaluators.name: role-fulfilment
evaluators.criteria:
The agent's role is: Lightweight in-session retriever. Runs fetch-context as the primary cross-source search, optionally enriches with embedding/memory tools when confidence is low, and returns structured JSON (status, confidence, results, context_summary, active_items) in under ~15s. Read-only, never conversational.
The user's request is given to the agent. Score how well the response ACTUALLY DOES the agent's job for that request, in the agent's voice. Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).

agent_tests.name: retrieval/fast_retrieval::pack:probe_calendar_event_lookup
agent_tests.prompt:
Fetch all context related to my master thesis defense scheduled for 05.03.2026 at 12:20 in room 103G, including any preparation tasks, suggested answers, and calendar events that were previously planned. I need to see what's already stored so I know what still needs to be done.

Reply with your actual response for THIS request only. Do not describe your role, do not simulate or role-play a user, do not narrate what you would do — just do it and return the result a user would receive.

evaluators.name: probe_calendar_event_lookup
evaluators.criteria:
Agent is a read-only in-session retriever returning structured JSON (status, confidence, results, context_summary, active_items). A good response searches cross-source for all data tied to the thesis defense (calendar events, task descriptions, preparation notes), returns results in structured JSON format within ~15s, does NOT attempt to modify anything or respond conversationally, and indicates confidence level for each retrieved item.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).

agent_tests.name: retrieval/fast_retrieval::pack:probe_stored_preferences_fetch
agent_tests.prompt:
Retrieve all stored preferences and configuration I've given you: which calendar is work (techtree.dev) vs personal (ignacy.daszkiewicz@gmail.com), my daily availability (not available until 18:00), and any other preference facts like what 'attach image to task' means to me. Return everything you can find.

Reply with your actual response for THIS request only. Do not describe your role, do not simulate or role-play a user, do not narrate what you would do — just do it and return the result a user would receive.

evaluators.name: probe_stored_preferences_fetch
evaluators.criteria:
Agent is a read-only cross-source retriever. A good response performs a broad search across memory/preferences sources for calendar assignments, scheduling constraints, and custom user definitions, returns structured JSON with each preference as a separate result, includes confidence scores, does not converse or confirm — only retrieves and returns data.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).

agent_tests.name: retrieval/fast_retrieval::pack:probe_polish_history_search
agent_tests.prompt:
Znajdź w historii naszych rozmów jak robiliśmy to wcześniej — szukałem informacji o rynku pracy, algorytmach, bezpieczeństwie informacji. Chcę żebyś sprawdził całą dostępną historię i wyciągnął powiązane notatki, podsumowania i fakty które wcześniej zapisaliśmy.

Reply with your actual response for THIS request only. Do not describe your role, do not simulate or role-play a user, do not narrate what you would do — just do it and return the result a user would receive.

evaluators.name: probe_polish_history_search
evaluators.criteria:
Agent is a non-conversational structured retriever. A good response runs cross-source search (fetch-context + optional embedding enrichment) for topics: labor market, algorithms, information security — across Polish and English content, returns structured JSON with status/confidence/results/context_summary/active_items, does NOT attempt to answer the question or generate new content, only retrieves existing stored data.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).

agent_tests.name: retrieval/fast_retrieval::pack:probe_research_topic_retrieval
agent_tests.prompt:
Get everything related to my AI agents research tracking: I previously mentioned I want to track new frameworks, tools, approaches to organizing work, autonomous coding agents, testing environments, business influence and applications for earning money, and new platforms. I also sent 3 videos from different YouTube channels for this topic. Retrieve all stored research items, tracked channels, and any analysis already done.

Reply with your actual response for THIS request only. Do not describe your role, do not simulate or role-play a user, do not narrate what you would do — just do it and return the result a user would receive.

evaluators.name: probe_research_topic_retrieval
evaluators.criteria:
Agent is a lightweight read-only retriever. A good response searches across research tracking storage, video/YouTube data, and topic entries for 'AI agents' research, returns structured JSON grouping results by subtopic (frameworks, tools, autonomous agents, testing, business applications, platforms), includes confidence per result, enriches with embedding search if initial confidence is low, completes in under ~15s.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).

agent_tests.name: retrieval/fast_retrieval::pack:probe_health_and_training_recall
agent_tests.prompt:
Fetch my stored training plan, any health-related notes I've asked you to remember (including my methylphenidate dosage — 10mg is my normal dose and it's not high for me), and any other 'remember for later' items that are still pending or unresolved. I want a full picture of what's stored about my routines and health.

Reply with your actual response for THIS request only. Do not describe your role, do not simulate or role-play a user, do not narrate what you would do — just do it and return the result a user would receive.

evaluators.name: probe_health_and_training_recall
evaluators.criteria:
Agent is a structured JSON-returning retriever, never conversational. A good response searches across memory/preference sources for training plans, medication notes, and pending reminders, returns each as a separate structured result with confidence score, distinguishes between confirmed facts (dosage is normal) and pending items (ask later reminders), does not offer to act on any of them — purely retrieves and returns data.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).

agent_tests.prompt:
What health-related things have I logged this spring — medication, test results, anything?

Reply with your actual response for THIS request only. Do not describe your role, do not simulate or role-play a user — just answer with the retrieved information.

evaluators.name: groundedness
evaluators.criteria: The answer must cite CONCRETE items retrieved from the user's actual history (e.g. medication doses such as MPH, the April ferritin blood result, dentist appointment) — not generic wellness advice. Score by groundedness: 1.0 = several specific retrieved items with details; 0.5 = one vague but real item; 0.0 = generic filler, refusal, or fabricated items.

agent_tests.name: retrieval:open:summarize_what_hardware_and_homelab_stuf
agent_tests.prompt:
Summarize what hardware and homelab stuff I've dealt with lately.

Reply with your actual response for THIS request only. Do not describe your role, do not simulate or role-play a user — just answer with the retrieved information.

evaluators.name: groundedness
evaluators.criteria: Must reference concrete retrieved specifics (e.g. server rack fan model, GPU/server maintenance, orange pi journal logger). Score by groundedness and specificity; 0.0 for invented hardware or generic talk about homelabs.

agent_tests.name: retrieval:open:co_ostatnio_planowa_em_kupi_albo_ju_kupi
agent_tests.prompt:
Co ostatnio planowałem kupić albo już kupiłem? Przypomnij mi konkrety.

Reply with your actual response for THIS request only. Do not describe your role, do not simulate or role-play a user — just answer with the retrieved information.

evaluators.name: groundedness
evaluators.criteria: Polish question about recent purchases. Must surface concrete retrieved purchases/plans (e.g. the Markus II chair for 899 zł, a second Noctua fan). Answer may be Polish or English; score on concrete retrieved facts, 0.0 for invented purchases.

agent_tests.prompt:
What appointments or travel do I have coming up, based on everything you know?

Reply with your actual response for THIS request only. Do not describe your role, do not simulate or role-play a user — just answer with the retrieved information.

evaluators.name: groundedness
evaluators.criteria: Must pull REAL stored items (e.g. dentist Dr Marlena Wójcik March 14th 11:30, flight LO1923 to Lisbon May 21st). Score 1.0 when the known stored appointments surface with their details; 0.0 for invented events or 'I don't know' without an attempt to search.

agent_tests.name: retrieval:journal:this_week
agent_tests.prompt:
What did I write in my digital journal in the last few days? Check the journal log, not just our chat.

Reply with your actual response for THIS request only. Do not describe your role, do not simulate or role-play a user — just answer with the retrieved information.

evaluators.name: journal-grounded
evaluators.criteria: The agent must have ACTUALLY consulted the journal/telegram log source (fetch-context). Score 1.0 if it reports concrete journal entries (or a truthful 'the journal has no entries this week' AFTER checking). Score 0.0 if it answers from chat history only, invents entries, or refuses without checking.

agent_tests.name: retrieval:selfexam:bike_lock_when
agent_tests.prompt:
When did I give you my bike lock code, and what was it? Find the actual message.

Reply with your actual response for THIS request only. Do not describe your role, do not simulate or role-play a user — just answer with the retrieved information.

agent_tests.name: retrieval:selfexam:past_sessions
agent_tests.prompt:
Look into your own recent agent sessions: what kinds of tasks have you been running lately? Use session inspection, then summarize honestly.

Reply with your actual response for THIS request only. Do not describe your role, do not simulate or role-play a user — just answer with the retrieved information.

evaluators.name: session-introspection
evaluators.criteria: The agent must inspect its own past sessions (session-inspector or equivalent) and report REAL observed sessions/tasks. Score 1.0 for a concrete, honest summary of actual recent sessions; 0.5 for a thin but real attempt; 0.0 for fabricated activity or refusing to introspect.

capability_tests.name: retrieval-is-json-only
capability_tests.description: Output must be structured JSON, not conversational prose.
